citations.narrative:
The following items are in need of repair:
* The floor of the African Porcupine's indoor housing has several areas of missing paint throughout. This floor is no longer
in good repair. No flakes of peeled paint were observed in the enclosure, but the missing areas of paint now expose the
cement floor. Due to the missing paint, the exposed cement floor is no longer in good repair.
The floor should be repaired to maintain it in good repair and to protect the porcupine from injury should paint be ingested.
* The wooden shelter, for the raccoon housed outdoors, has rotted and deteriorated wood around the base and on the
exterior of the entrance door. The wood on the door is detaching from the door's framework. These damaged areas could
cause injury to the raccoon housed therein.
The damaged areas of this shelter building should be repaired to maintain it in good repair and to protect the raccoon
from injury.
The facility must be constructed of such material and of such strength as appropriate for the animals involved. The indoor
and outdoor housing facilities shall be structurally sound and shall be maintained in good repair to protect the animals
from injury and to contain the animals.

The feed storage building has metal drums with lids to protect the supplies of animal food. One bag of unopened Mazuri
Exotic Animal Feed was stored on the lid of one of these containers. The bag had a small hole in it, on the side at the
letter "a" in the word Mazuri. Feed is visible through said hole. Loose feed, along with mouse droppings were present on
this lid adjacent to the bag.
Additionally, loose feed and mouse droppings were present on the lid of the drum listed as small bird diets and mouse
droppings were present on the lid of the drum listed as parrot food.
Mouse droppings and loose feed are indicative of the presence of mice.
Facility should update their pest control program to ensure that pests, such as mice, do not destroy unopened bags of
feed or leave droppings on top of feed containers.
A safe and effective program for the control of insects, ectoparasites, and avian and mammalian pests shall be
established and maintained.

The Blue & Gold macaw and Moluccan cockatoo are housed adjacent to each other, sharing the same cement floor. The
paint has peeled away, thus exposing the cement throughout. No peeled paint flakes were observed. This missing paint
now leaves the floor in disrepair. Peeled paint flakes, should they appear, could harm the birds if ingested.
Facility should repair the floor to maintain it in good repair and to protect the birds from injury.
Housing facilities for birds must be designed and constructed so that they are structurally sound for the species of bird
housed in them. They must be kept in good repair, protect the birds from injury, and restrict other animals from entering
that may negatively affect the welfare of the birds within.
TO BE CORRECTED BY: MAY 10, 2026
This inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the facility representatives.
n

narrative:
3.125(a)  Repeat
   
Facilities, general. 
The following items are in need of repair: 
 
* The floor of the African Porcupine's indoor housing has several areas of missing paint throughout. This floor is no longer 
in good repair. No flakes of peeled paint were observed in the enclosure, but the missing areas of paint now expose the 
cement floor. Due to the missing paint, the exposed cement floor is no longer in good repair. 
 
The floor should be repaired to maintain it in good repair and to protect the porcupine from injury should paint be ingested. 
 
* The wooden shelter, for the raccoon housed outdoors, has rotted and deteriorated wood around the base and on the 
exterior of the entrance door. The wood on the door is detaching from the door's framework. These damaged areas could 
cause injury to the raccoon housed therein. 
 
The damaged areas of this shelter building should be repaired to maintain it in good repair and to protect the raccoon 
from injury. 
 
The facility must be constructed of such material and of such strength as appropriate for the animals involved. The indoor 
and outdoor housing facilities shall be structurally sound and shall be maintained in good repair to protect the animals 
from injury and to contain the animals. 
3.131(d)  Repeat
   
Sanitation. 
The feed storage building has metal drums with lids to protect the supplies of animal food. One bag of unopened Mazuri 
Exotic Animal Feed was stored on the lid of one of these containers. The bag had a small hole in it, on the side at the 
letter "a" in the word Mazuri. Feed is visible through said hole. Loose feed, along with mouse droppings were present on 
this lid adjacent to the bag.  
 
Additionally, loose feed and mouse droppings were present on the lid of the drum listed as small bird diets and mouse 
droppings were present on the lid of the drum listed as parrot food.

Mouse droppings and loose feed are indicative of the presence of mice. 
 
Facility should update their pest control program to ensure that pests, such as mice, do not destroy unopened bags of 
feed or leave droppings on top of feed containers. 
 
A safe and effective program for the control of insects, ectoparasites, and avian and mammalian pests shall be 
established and maintained. 
3.150(a) 
   
Facilities, general. 
The Blue & Gold macaw and Moluccan cockatoo are housed adjacent to each other, sharing the same cement floor. The 
paint has peeled away, thus exposing the cement throughout. No peeled paint flakes were observed. This missing paint 
now leaves the floor in disrepair. Peeled paint flakes, should they appear, could harm the birds if ingested. 
 
Facility should repair the floor to maintain it in good repair and to protect the birds from injury. 
 
Housing facilities for birds must be designed and constructed so that they are structurally sound for the species of bird 
housed in them. They must be kept in good repair, protect the birds from injury, and restrict other animals from entering 
that may negatively affect the welfare of the birds within. 
 
TO BE CORRECTED BY: MAY 10, 2026 
 
This inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the facility representatives.
